comments.comment: 191 tonnes of solid waste was incinerated onsite using a mass burn excess air incinerator for 2020. Emission factors for part 3 substances were not provided in AP-42 Chapter 2.1 for mass burn excess air incinerators (i.e., no information available was selected).

comments.comment: The tailings material contain total aluminum rather than aluminum (fume or dust only).

type.en: Reasons for excluding quantities in tailings or waste rock
type.fr: Raisons pour exclure les quantités dans les résidus miniers ou dans les stériles

comments.comment: The waste rock sulfur content is 0.28% and the ratio of neutralizing potential to acid generating potential is 7:1 which is greater than 3:1. Additionally, the arsenic concentration in waste rock is 9 mg/kg, which is less than 12 mg/kg. Therefore, the waste rock disposal at the Red Chris Mine is exempt from reporting to the NPRI.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Approximately 302 tonnes of MSW was incinerated on site in 2022, which is greater than the 26 tonne threshold. Dioxins and furans - total emissions were updated using a new emission factor.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: 301,800 kg incinerator throughput in 2022. Dioxins and furans - total emissions were updated using a new emission factor.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: No Dioxins and furans - total were disposed.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: No Dioxins and furans - total were recycled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Unpaved road emissions comprise over 80% of all TPM emissions.  Unpaved road emissions are a function of the site vehicle fleet  and the site vehicle fleet was updated in 2022.

Point sources or stack emissions at the facility emit particulate matter as a result of material handling (e.g., conveying, crushing, etc.), combustion of fuels (i.e., propane, diesel and gasoline) and combustion in municipal solid waste.

For combustion of diesel and gasoline, the emission factors used do not differentiate between condensable and filterable particulates; therefore, it is unknown if these particulate estimates include condensable particulates.  For all other sources and activities only filterable particulates are provided.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Unpaved road emissions comprise over 80% of all PM10 emissions.  Unpaved road emissions are a function of the site vehicle fleet  and the site vehicle fleet was updated in 2022.

Point sources or stack emissions at the facility emit particulate matter as a result of material handling (e.g., conveying, crushing, etc.), combustion of fuels (i.e., propane, diesel and gasoline) and combustion in municipal solid waste.

For combustion of diesel and gasoline, the emission factors used do not differentiate between condensable and filterable particulates; therefore, it is unknown if these particulate estimates include condensable particulates.  For all other sources and activities only filterable particulates are provided.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Unpaved road emissions comprise over 50% of all PM2.5 emissions.  Unpaved road emissions are a function of the site vehicle fleet  and the site vehicle fleet was updated in 2022.

Point sources or stack emissions at the facility emit particulate matter as a result of material handling (e.g., conveying, crushing, etc.), combustion of fuels (i.e., propane, diesel and gasoline) and combustion in municipal solid waste.

For combustion of diesel and gasoline, the emission factors used do not differentiate between condensable and filterable particulates; therefore, it is unknown if these particulate estimates include condensable particulates.  For all other sources and activities only filterable particulates are provided.
